rationale: Ecology & Evolution is the smallest of the natural-science level-1 categories I added, but it has rich internal structure. Twelve subspecialties split by scale (population, community, ecosystem), by lens (evolution vs. ecology), and by environment (terrestrial vs. marine vs. freshwater). I made Paleobiology its own card to capture deep-time work that doesn't fit cleanly under Geology. Biodiversity & Conservation gets its own card because the policy-relevant conservation community is large and identity-distinct.

subspecialties.name: Evolutionary Biology
covers: adaptive evolution, speciation, phylogenetics, evolutionary theory, evolutionary developmental biology (evo-devo), molecular evolution
Evolution, Mol Biol Evol

subspecialties.name: Population Ecology
covers: population dynamics, demography, life-history evolution, density-dependence, metapopulation, age-structured models
Ecology, J Anim Ecol

subspecialties.name: Community Ecology
subspecialties.tagline: Species interactions, biodiversity, food webs, assembly.
covers: species interactions, food webs, biodiversity patterns, community assembly, ecological networks, mutualism
Ecology Letters, Oikos

subspecialties.name: Behavioral Ecology
subspecialties.tagline: Animal behavior, social evolution, mating, foraging.
covers: animal behavior in ecological context, mating systems, foraging theory, social evolution, animal communication, sexual selection
Behav Ecol, Anim Behav
explicitly_excludes: neuroscience of behavior → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Macroecology & Biogeography
subspecialties.tagline: Large-scale patterns, species ranges, latitudinal gradients.
covers: macroecological patterns, biogeography, species range modeling, latitudinal diversity, island biogeography, beta diversity

subspecialties.name: Conservation Biology & Biodiversity
subspecialties.tagline: Extinction risk, protected areas, conservation genetics.
covers: extinction risk assessment, protected area design, conservation genetics, species recovery, conservation planning, biodiversity policy science
Conserv Biol, Biol Conserv

subspecialties.name: Global Change Ecology
subspecialties.tagline: Climate impact on species, range shifts, novel ecosystems.
covers: climate-change impacts on species and communities, range shifts, phenological shifts, novel ecosystems, ecological tipping points
Glob Change Biol ecology, Nat Clim Change ecol

subspecialties.name: Microbial Ecology
covers: environmental microbial communities, soil microbiome, marine microbiome, microbial diversity patterns, microbe-driven biogeochemistry
ISME J, Environ Microbiol

subspecialties.name: Disease Ecology & Eco-Epidemiology
subspecialties.tagline: Wildlife disease, vector ecology, zoonosis ecology.
covers: wildlife disease, vector ecology, zoonotic disease ecology, ecological drivers of emergence
Ecol Lett disease, Phil Trans B disease ecology

subspecialties.name: Paleobiology & Paleontology
covers: fossil record analysis, macroevolutionary patterns, mass extinctions, taphonomy as paleobiology, ancient DNA evolution
Paleobiology, J Vert Paleontol

subspecialties.name: Marine & Freshwater Ecology
covers: coral reef ecology, kelp ecosystems, fisheries science, freshwater ecology, estuarine ecology, ocean ecology
Mar Ecol Prog Ser, Limnol Oceanogr

subspecialties.name: Ecological Theory & Modeling
covers: mathematical ecology, food-web theory, ABM in ecology, niche theory, neutral theory, network ecology theory
Theor Ecol, Am Nat theoretical

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Behavior, Cognition & Ecology — folded under Behavioral Ecology.
Considered Restoration Ecology as its own card — small; folds under Conservation Biology.
Considered Urban Ecology — folds under Community or Global Change Ecology.
